title: Seeded Simulation, Input Replay, State Hashes, Settings, and Save Migration · Program Brief: State Hash
summary: Rendering consumes random draws, replay stores key state without timing, floats are compared as formatted text, settings overwrite atomically unsafe, and corrupted saves silently reset. Predict player-visible behavior, state, units, timing, input, rendering, feedback, failure, accessibility, and evidence before reading the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the state hash increment in reproducible replay and versioned local state. Learners progress through prediction, explicit t…
objectives: Canonicalize relevant simulation state and compare tolerant numeric fields without including display-only noise.
Record simulation-step actions, seed, version, settings, viewport policy, and checkpoints rather than device events alone.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-asteroids-python cumulative accessible real-time game portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python simulation models and fixed fixtures only.
Native pygame-ce, SDL, windows, controllers, audio devices, assets, files, installers,
GPU behavior, frame pacing, accessibility playtests, packaging, and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class SimulationPolicy:
    fixed_step_seconds: float = 1 / 120
    max_frame_seconds: float = 0.1
    max_updates_per_frame: int = 8
    logical_width: int = 960
    logical_height: int = 540


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class RunEvidence:
    revision: str
    seed: int
    input_trace_id: str
    runtime_id: str
    accessibility_profile: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python Asteroids portfolio ready"

steps.title: Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Predict State Hash · Theory State Hash
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory Asteroids team handles case 6623 during a low-vision laptop playtest at 125 percent scaling. Build deterministic Python evidence to separate seeded randomness and input frames from rendering so replay, save migration, and corruption rejection are reproducible; test empty, zero-vector, seam, low-frame-rate, focus-loss, device-loss, missing-asset, corrupted-save, and rejected cases; then replay the same input trace at 30, 60, and 144 presentation frames per second and compare simulation state. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real pygame-ce, SDL, windows, controllers, audio devices, assets, files, installers, frame timing, GPUs, packaging, and production behavior require explicit controlled desktop transfer gates. Competency probe: Canonicalize relevant simulation state and compare tolerant numeric fields without including display-only noise.

content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: A worked example Asteroids team handles case 6623 during a low-vision laptop playtest at 125 percent scaling. Build deterministic Python evidence to separate seeded randomness and input frames from rendering so replay, save migration, and corruption rejection are reproducible; test empty, zero-vector, seam, low-frame-rate, focus-loss, device-loss, missing-asset, corrupted-save, and rejected cases; then replay the same input trace at 30, 60, and 144 presentation frames per second and compare simulation state. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real pygame-ce, SDL, windows, controllers, audio devices, assets, files, installers, frame timing, GPUs, packaging, and production behavior require explicit controlled desktop transfer gates. Competency probe: Canonicalize relevant simulation state and compare tolerant numeric fields without including display-only noise. A defensible solution applies State Hash, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to reproducible replay and versioned local state.

content.code:
# Evidence: asteroids-worked-asteroids-determinism-replay-save-4
# Competency: asteroids-worked-asteroids-determinism-replay-save-4.
# Case 1655: a low-vision laptop playtest at 125 percent scaling.
# Operating constraint: test empty, zero-vector, seam, low-frame-rate, focus-loss, device-loss, missing-asset, corrupted-save, and rejected cases.
# Changed case: inject a 400 millisecond frame stall and prove the update budget prevents a spiral of death.
def worked_asteroids_determinism_replay_save_4(seed: int = 4217, input_frames: int = 3600, replay_frames: int = 3600, state_hash_equal: bool = True, schema_version: int = 2, corrupted_save_rejected: bool = True) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_worked4 = "worked4-4dekrow-1655"
    assert evidence_variant_worked4
    evidence_axes_worked4 = (
        "playerworked4",
        "seed1655",
        "input4dekrow",
        "frameworked41655",
        "state1655worked4",
        "playtest4dekrow1655",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_worked4)) == 6
    if seed < 0 or input_frames < 1:
        return False, input_frames, "replay-domain"
    if replay_frames != input_frames:
        return False, replay_frames, "replay-length"
    if not state_hash_equal:
        return False, replay_frames, "simulation-drift"
    if schema_version < 1 or not corrupted_save_rejected:
        return False, schema_version, "save-contract"
    assert replay_frames == input_frames
    return True, schema_version, "reproducible-run"

content.code:
# Evidence: asteroids-state-hash-121wey
# Competency: asteroids-state-hash.
# Case 2700: a controller-remapping accessibility review.
# Operating constraint: keep every critical cue available through more than color or sound and every action remappable without timing precision.
# Changed case: cross both screen seams in one update and retain collision and rendering continuity.
def evidence_asteroids_state_hash_121wey(seed: int = 4217, input_frames: int = 3600, replay_frames: int = 3600, state_hash_equal: bool = True, schema_version: int = 2, corrupted_save_rejected: bool = True) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_121wey = "121wey-yew121-2700"
    assert evidence_variant_121wey
    evidence_axes_121wey = (
        "player121wey",
        "seed2700",
        "inputyew121",
        "frame121wey2700",
        "state2700121wey",
        "playtestyew1212700",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_121wey)) == 6
    if seed < 0 or input_frames < 1:
        return False, input_frames, "replay-domain"
    if replay_frames != input_frames:
        return False, replay_frames, "replay-length"
    if not state_hash_equal:
        return False, replay_frames, "simulation-drift"
    if schema_version < 1 or not corrupted_save_rejected:
        return False, schema_version, "save-contract"
    assert replay_frames == input_frames
    return True, schema_version, "reproducible-run"

steps.instruction:
Write the theory evidence note for State Hash: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A theory Asteroids team handles case 5825 during a low-vision laptop playtest at 125 percent scaling. Build deterministic Python evidence to separate seeded randomness and input frames from rendering so replay, save migration, and corruption rejection are reproducible; keep browser work pure, deterministic, bounded, and free of native display, device, audio, file, package, and operating-system effects; then cross both screen seams in one update and retain collision and rendering continuity. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real pygame-ce, SDL, windows, controllers, audio devices, assets, files, installers, frame timing, GPUs, packaging, and production behavior require explicit controlled desktop transfer gates. Competency probe: Canonicalize relevant simulation state and compare tolerant numeric fields without including display-only noise.

Evidence target: Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Defend the State Hash model · Theory State Hash.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide. For Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Defend the State Hash model · Theory State Hash, keep State Hash connected to executable Python game-state, timing, input, collision, accessibility, test, package, and release decision evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Canonicalize relevant simulation state and compare tolerant numeric fields without including display-only noise.

hints: For Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Defend the State Hash model · Theory State Hash, name the player, revision, runtime, seed, input, time, coordinate, state, entity, collision, render, cue, accessibility, package, release, and desktop-transfer boundary, State Hash precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Defend the State Hash model · Theory State Hash, trace this concrete input and predict event, intent, simulation, collision, cleanup, rendering, cue, accessibility, failure, pause, rollback, recovery, and desktop evidence before reviewing it: A theory Asteroids team handles case 5825 during a low-vision laptop playtest at 125 percent scaling. Build deterministic Python evidence to separate seeded randomness and input frames from rendering so replay, save migration, and corruption rejection are reproducible; keep browser work pure, deterministic, bounded, and free of native display, device, audio, file, package, and operating-system effects; then cross both screen seams in one update and retain collision and rendering continuity. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real pygame-ce, SDL, windows, controllers, audio devices, assets, files, installers, frame timing, GPUs, packaging, and production behavior require explicit controlled desktop transfer gates. Competency probe: Canonicalize relevant simulation state and compare tolerant numeric fields without including display-only noise.

For Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Defend the State Hash model · Theory State Hash, challenge the documented misconception with changed-frame-rate, stall, seam, input, device, collision, cue, accessibility, save, package, failure, recovery, and controlled desktop-transfer evidence: Hashing repr of every object creates a stable replay checksum.

checks.description: The cumulative python portfolio contains this competency’s named evidence increment.
checks.failureMessage: Add the exact evidence marker # Evidence: asteroids-state-hash-121wey.

checks.pattern: # Evidence: asteroids-state-hash-121wey(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?def\s+evidence_asteroids_state_hash_121wey\s*\((?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?\)\s*(?:->[^:]+)?\s*:(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?replay_frames != input_frames)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?not state_hash_equal)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?schema_version < 1)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?not corrupted_save_rejected)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?assert)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?return)(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?return
